response.justification: The candidate is an experienced full-stack software engineer with strong web, mobile, and some embedded background, but the resume shows no evidence of SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator work, deep-learning workload optimization, GPU architecture, SIMD/MIMD experience, or hardware-software co-design. The role is a Principal Performance Architect focused on silicon and AI accelerator performance, which is a fundamentally different discipline from this candidate's application/services background. Education meets the BS + 8 years bar, but the technical domain mismatch is decisive.
strengths: Long, varied engineering career (~14+ years) across web, mobile, embedded, and dev tooling
Some low-level/systems exposure via DLang, C/C++ at National Instruments, and high-throughput services at Sociomantic
Demonstrated ability to learn new stacks and operate as a senior IC across domains
concerns: No SoC, IP modeling, or hardware architecture experience listed
No AI accelerator, deep-learning training/inference, or GPU workload optimization background
No SIMD/MIMD, hardware-software co-design, or performance-architecture work at the silicon level

key_factors.factor: Lack of SoC/IP modeling and hardware architecture experience
No AI accelerator or deep-learning workload optimization background
Senior-level software engineering breadth and longevity
